title: Desired Capabilities
overview: Configure Appium sessions using DesiredCapabilities or Options objects. Required capabilities: platformName, deviceName, automationName (UiAutomator2/XCUITest), app (APK/IPA path or app package).
whyItMatters: Without capabilities, Appium doesn't know which device, platform, or app to automate. Correct configuration is the foundation of any mobile automation script.
explanation: Desired Capabilities are JSON objects sent by the client to the server when requesting a new automation session. In Appium 2.0, capabilities are structured using W3C standards (e.g., `appium:deviceName`). Common properties include `platformName` (Android/iOS), `appium:automationName` (UiAutomator2, XCUITest), and app-specific caps like `appium:appPackage` and `appium:appActivity`. You can use `UiAutomator2Options` and `XCUITestOptions` classes in modern Appium Java bindings instead of plain `DesiredCapabilities` to ensure type safety.
keyPoints: W3C capability format is mandatory in Appium 2 (prefix vendor-specific caps with `appium:`).
Essential caps: platformName, automationName, deviceName, app/appPackage.
iOS specifics: udid, bundleId, xcodeOrgId, xcodeSigningId.
realWorldExample: When running tests on a device farm like BrowserStack, you pass a vast array of custom capabilities to define the OS version, device model, and debugging options like capturing video and network logs.
commonMistakes: Not using the `appium:` prefix for Appium 2 vendor capabilities.
Setting `app` (the APK/IPA) when the app is already installed (should use package/bundle ID instead).
bestPractices: Externalize capabilities into a configuration file (.properties or JSON) to switch devices easily.
Use typed Options classes (e.g. UiAutomator2Options) rather than raw DesiredCapabilities.
Keep Appium server logs handy to debug session creation failures.

interviewQuestions.q: What is the difference between DesiredCapabilities and Options classes in Appium?
interviewQuestions.a: DesiredCapabilities is a generic key-value map, whereas Options classes (like UiAutomator2Options) are platform-specific and type-safe, preventing typos and enforcing W3C compliance.

interviewQuestions.q: What are the required capabilities to launch an Android app in Appium 2?
interviewQuestions.a: platformName, appium:automationName (e.g. UiAutomator2), and either appium:app (path to APK) or appium:appPackage/appActivity.

revisionSummary: Desired Capabilities configure the Appium session. In Appium 2, use W3C format (`appium:` prefix) and type-safe Options classes (UiAutomator2Options/XCUITestOptions) to define platform, device, and app details.
